name: socket-request

metadata.name: socket-request
metadata.summary: socket_request is building and parsing HTTP requests with UNIX domain sockets.

documentation:
# socket_request

socket_request is build and parses HTTP requests with unix domain sockets.

### Installing socket_request

socket_request is available on PyPI:

```
pip3 install socket-request

```
Officially supports Python 3.7+.



### Examples:

```python
import socket_request

docker = socket_request.DockerSock(unix_socket="/var/run/docker.sock")
docker.get_docker_images()

```


```python
import socket_request

control_chain = socket_request.ControlChain(unix_socket="./cli.sock")
control_chain.stop()
control_chain.start()

```

```angular2html
Command Line Interface for control_chain

positional arguments:
    {start,stop,reset,leave,view_chain,view_system_config,join,backup,backup_list,restore,chain_config,system_config,ls,prune}

optional arguments:
  -h, --help            show this help message and exit

  -w wait_state, --wait-state wait_state
                        wait_state (default: True)

                        wait_state (default: True)

                        auto_prepare (default: True)
  -p payload_file, --payload payload_file
                        payload file
  -f, --forever         retry forever
  -i, --inspect         inspect for view chain
  --seedAddress SEEDADDRESS
                        seed list string
  -b BASE_DIR, --base-dir BASE_DIR
                        base dir for goloop
  -pd payload dict, --payload-dict payload dict
                        payload dict
  -bh BLOCKHEIGHT, --blockheight BLOCKHEIGHT
    BlockHeight for pruning
  -rn RESTORE_NAME, --restore-name RESTORE_NAME
    Restore filename for restore 
```

## ControlChain
Parent class is ConnectSock class

classes.name: socket_request.utils.data.RequestField

methods.name: socket_request.utils.data.RequestField.from_tuples
documentation:
A {class}`~urllib3.fields.RequestField` factory from old-style tuple parameters.

Supports constructing {class}`~urllib3.fields.RequestField` from
parameter of key/value strings AND key/filetuple. A filetuple is a
(filename, data, MIME type) tuple where the MIME type is optional.
For example:

```
'foo': 'bar',
'fakefile': ('foofile.txt', 'contents of foofile'),
'realfile': ('barfile.txt', open('realfile').read()),
'typedfile': ('bazfile.bin', open('bazfile').read(), 'image/jpeg'),
'nonamefile': 'contents of nonamefile field',
```

Field names and filenames must be unicode.

methods.name: socket_request.utils.data.RequestField._render_part
documentation:
Overridable helper function to format a single header parameter. By
default, this calls `self.header_formatter`.

```
param name

The name of the parameter, a string expected to be ASCII only.

param value

The value of the parameter, provided as a unicode string.
```

methods.name: socket_request.utils.data.RequestField._render_parts
documentation:
Helper function to format and quote a single header.

Useful for single headers that are composed of multiple items. E.g.,
'Content-Disposition' fields.

```
param header_parts

A sequence of (k, v) tuples or a  of (k, v) to format
as .
```

methods.name: socket_request.utils.data.RequestField.make_multipart
documentation:
Makes this request field into a multipart request field.

This method overrides "Content-Disposition", "Content-Type" and
"Content-Location" headers to the request parameter.

```
param content_type

The 'Content-Type' of the request body.

param content_location

The 'Content-Location' of the request body.
```

A data container for request body parameters.

```
param name

The name of this request field. Must be unicode.

param data

The data/value body.

param filename

An optional filename of the request field. Must be unicode.

param headers

An optional dict-like object of headers to initially use for the field.
```

functions.name: socket_request.utils.utils.dict_to_line
documentation:
Converts a dictionary into a string with various formatting options, automatically quoting string values if desired.

```
param dict_param

The dictionary to convert.

param quotes

If True, wraps string values in quotes.

param separator

The separator between keys and values.

param end_separator

The separator between key-value pairs.

param pad_width

The minimum width for value alignment.

param key_pad_width

The minimum width for key alignment.

param alignment

The alignment of the values ('left', 'right', 'center').

param key_alignment

The alignment of the keys ('left', 'right', 'center').

param callback

An optional callback function to apply to each value.

return

The formatted string.
```
